1: // ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
2: // MODULE: Code Graph Recovery Procedures
3: // ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
4: // Typed implementations of CG-RP-001, CG-RP-002, and CG-RP-003.
5: 
6: import Database from 'better-sqlite3';
7: import {
8:   copyFileSync,
9:   existsSync,
10:   mkdirSync,
11:   readdirSync,
12:   renameSync,
13:   rmSync,
14: } from 'node:fs';
15: import { basename, join, relative, resolve, sep } from 'node:path';
16: import { DATABASE_DIR } from '../core/config.js';
17: import { closeDb } from './code-graph-db.js';
18: 
19: export type ScanFunction = (args: { incremental: boolean }) => Promise<unknown>;
20: 
21: export interface RecoveryProcedureOptions {
22:   dbDir?: string;
23:   auditDir?: string;
24:   now?: () => Date;
25:   scan?: ScanFunction;
26: }
27: 
28: export interface RecoveryProcedureResult {
29:   procedureId: 'CG-RP-001' | 'CG-RP-002' | 'CG-RP-003';
30:   status: 'ok' | 'failed';
31:   quarantineDir?: string;
32:   recoveryDir?: string;
33:   knownGoodDir?: string;
34:   restored?: boolean;
35:   scanIncremental?: boolean;
36:   integrityCheck?: string;
37:   stagedFiles?: string[];
38:   errors: string[];
39: }
40: 
41: const DB_TRIPLET = [
42:   'code-graph.sqlite',
43:   'code-graph.sqlite-wal',
44:   'code-graph.sqlite-shm',
45: ] as const;
46: 
47: function timestamp(now: () => Date): string {
48:   return now().toISOString().replace(/[:.]/g, '-');
49: }
50: 
51: function ensureDir(path: string): string {
52:   mkdirSync(path, { recursive: true, mode: 0o700 });
53:   return path;
54: }
55: 
56: function assertInside(parent: string, child: string): void {
57:   const parentResolved = resolve(parent);
58:   const childResolved = resolve(child);
59:   const parentWithSep = parentResolved.endsWith(sep) ? parentResolved : `${parentResolved}${sep}`;
60:   if (childResolved !== parentResolved && !childResolved.startsWith(parentWithSep)) {
61:     throw new Error(`Path escapes recovery root: ${child}`);
62:   }
63: }
64: 
65: function resolveDbDir(dbDir?: string): string {
66:   const resolved = resolve(dbDir ?? DATABASE_DIR);
67:   ensureDir(resolved);
68:   return resolved;
69: }
70: 
71: function tripletPath(dbDir: string, fileName: typeof DB_TRIPLET[number]): string {
72:   const path = join(dbDir, fileName);
73:   if (basename(path) !== fileName) {
74:     throw new Error(`Invalid DB triplet file name: ${fileName}`);
75:   }
76:   assertInside(dbDir, path);
77:   return path;
78: }
79: 
80: function copyTriplet(dbDir: string, targetDir: string): number {
81:   ensureDir(targetDir);
82:   assertInside(resolve(targetDir, '..'), targetDir);
83:   let copied = 0;
84:   for (const fileName of DB_TRIPLET) {
85:     const source = tripletPath(dbDir, fileName);
86:     if (!existsSync(source)) {
87:       continue;
88:     }
89:     copyFileSync(source, join(targetDir, fileName));
90:     copied += 1;
91:   }
92:   return copied;
93: }
94: 
95: function moveTriplet(dbDir: string, targetDir: string): number {
96:   ensureDir(targetDir);
97:   let moved = 0;
98:   for (const fileName of DB_TRIPLET) {
99:     const source = tripletPath(dbDir, fileName);
100:     if (!existsSync(source)) {
101:       continue;
102:     }
103:     renameSync(source, join(targetDir, fileName));
104:     moved += 1;
105:   }
106:   return moved;
107: }
108: 
109: function restoreTriplet(sourceDir: string, dbDir: string): number {
110:   let restored = 0;
111:   for (const fileName of DB_TRIPLET) {
112:     const source = join(sourceDir, fileName);
113:     if (!existsSync(source)) {
114:       continue;
115:     }
116:     copyFileSync(source, tripletPath(dbDir, fileName));
117:     restored += 1;
118:   }
119:   return restored;
120: }
121: 
122: function runIntegrityCheck(sqlitePath: string): string {
123:   if (!existsSync(sqlitePath)) {
124:     return 'missing';
125:   }
126:   const db = new Database(sqlitePath, { readonly: true, fileMustExist: true });
127:   try {
128:     const rows = db.pragma('integrity_check') as Array<Record<string, unknown>>;
129:     return rows.map((row) => String(Object.values(row)[0] ?? '')).join('\n') || 'ok';
130:   } finally {
131:     db.close();
132:   }
133: }
134: 
135: function findLatestKnownGood(dbDir: string, auditDir: string): string | null {
136:   const roots = [auditDir, dbDir];
137:   const candidates: string[] = [];
138:   for (const root of roots) {
139:     if (!existsSync(root)) {
140:       continue;
141:     }
142:     for (const entry of readdirSync(root, { withFileTypes: true })) {
143:       if (entry.isDirectory() && entry.name.startsWith('known-good-')) {
144:         candidates.push(join(root, entry.name));
145:       }
146:     }
147:   }
148:   return candidates.sort().at(-1) ?? null;
149: }
150: 
151: export function snapshotKnownGoodTriplet(options: RecoveryProcedureOptions = {}): string {
152:   const dbDir = resolveDbDir(options.dbDir);
153:   const auditDir = ensureDir(resolve(options.auditDir ?? join(dbDir, 'apply-audit')));
154:   assertInside(dbDir, auditDir);
155:   const knownGoodDir = join(auditDir, `known-good-${timestamp(options.now ?? (() => new Date()))}`);
156:   copyTriplet(dbDir, knownGoodDir);
157:   return knownGoodDir;
158: }
159: 
160: export async function recoverSqliteCorruption(
161:   options: RecoveryProcedureOptions = {},
162: ): Promise<RecoveryProcedureResult> {
163:   const dbDir = resolveDbDir(options.dbDir);
164:   const ts = timestamp(options.now ?? (() => new Date()));
165:   const recoveryDir = join(dbDir, `recovery-${ts}`);
166:   const quarantineDir = join(dbDir, `quarantine-${ts}`);
167:   const errors: string[] = [];
168:   let integrityCheck = 'missing';
169: 
170:   try {
171:     closeDb();
172:     copyTriplet(dbDir, recoveryDir);
173:     integrityCheck = runIntegrityCheck(join(recoveryDir, 'code-graph.sqlite'));
174:     moveTriplet(dbDir, quarantineDir);
175:     if (options.scan) {
176:       await options.scan({ incremental: false });
177:     }
178:     return {
179:       procedureId: 'CG-RP-001',
180:       status: 'ok',
181:       recoveryDir,
182:       quarantineDir,
183:       integrityCheck,
184:       scanIncremental: false,
185:       errors,
186:     };
187:   } catch (error) {
188:     errors.push(error instanceof Error ? error.message : String(error));
189:     return {
190:       procedureId: 'CG-RP-001',
191:       status: 'failed',
192:       recoveryDir,
193:       quarantineDir,
194:       integrityCheck,
195:       errors,
196:     };
197:   }
198: }
199: 
200: export async function recoverPartialScanFailure(
201:   options: RecoveryProcedureOptions & {
202:     staleFileCount?: number;
203:     gitHeadDrift?: boolean;
204:     schemaOrErrorSignal?: boolean;
205:   } = {},
206: ): Promise<RecoveryProcedureResult> {
207:   const dbDir = resolveDbDir(options.dbDir);
208:   const errors: string[] = [];
209:   const sqlitePath = tripletPath(dbDir, 'code-graph.sqlite');
210:   let integrityCheck = 'missing';
211:   let stagedFiles: string[] = [];
212: 
213:   try {
214:     integrityCheck = runIntegrityCheck(sqlitePath);
215:     if (existsSync(sqlitePath)) {
216:       const db = new Database(sqlitePath, { readonly: true, fileMustExist: true });
217:       try {
218:         const rows = db.prepare('SELECT file_path FROM code_files WHERE file_mtime_ms = 0 ORDER BY file_path LIMIT 50').all() as Array<{ file_path: string }>;
219:         stagedFiles = rows.map((row) => row.file_path);
220:       } finally {
221:         db.close();
222:       }
223:     }
224:     const staleFileCount = options.staleFileCount ?? stagedFiles.length;
225:     const incremental = staleFileCount <= 50 && options.gitHeadDrift !== true && options.schemaOrErrorSignal !== true;
226:     if (options.scan) {
227:       await options.scan({ incremental });
228:     }
229:     return {
230:       procedureId: 'CG-RP-002',
231:       status: 'ok',
232:       integrityCheck,
233:       stagedFiles,
234:       scanIncremental: incremental,
235:       errors,
236:     };
237:   } catch (error) {
238:     errors.push(error instanceof Error ? error.message : String(error));
239:     return {
240:       procedureId: 'CG-RP-002',
241:       status: 'failed',
242:       integrityCheck,
243:       stagedFiles,
244:       errors,
245:     };
246:   }
247: }
248: 
249: export async function rollbackBadApply(
250:   options: RecoveryProcedureOptions = {},
251: ): Promise<RecoveryProcedureResult> {
252:   const dbDir = resolveDbDir(options.dbDir);
253:   const auditDir = ensureDir(resolve(options.auditDir ?? join(dbDir, 'apply-audit')));
254:   assertInside(dbDir, auditDir);
255:   const ts = timestamp(options.now ?? (() => new Date()));
256:   const quarantineDir = join(auditDir, `bad-apply-${ts}`);
257:   const errors: string[] = [];
258:   let knownGoodDir: string | null = null;
259:   let restored = false;
260: 
261:   try {
262:     closeDb();
263:     moveTriplet(dbDir, quarantineDir);
264:     knownGoodDir = findLatestKnownGood(dbDir, auditDir);
265:     if (knownGoodDir) {
266:       assertInside(dbDir, knownGoodDir);
267:       restored = restoreTriplet(knownGoodDir, dbDir) > 0;
268:     }
269:     if (restored && existsSync(tripletPath(dbDir, 'code-graph.sqlite'))) {
270:       const db = new Database(tripletPath(dbDir, 'code-graph.sqlite'));
271:       try {
272:         db.pragma('wal_checkpoint(TRUNCATE)');
273:       } finally {
274:         db.close();
275:       }
276:     }
277:     if (options.scan) {
278:       await options.scan({ incremental: false });
279:     }
280:     return {
281:       procedureId: 'CG-RP-003',
282:       status: 'ok',
283:       quarantineDir,
284:       ...(knownGoodDir ? { knownGoodDir } : {}),
285:       restored,
286:       scanIncremental: false,
287:       errors,
288:     };
289:   } catch (error) {
290:     errors.push(error instanceof Error ? error.message : String(error));
291:     try {
292:       if (existsSync(quarantineDir) && readdirSync(quarantineDir).length === 0) {
293:         rmSync(quarantineDir, { recursive: true, force: true });
294:       }
295:     } catch {
296:       // Cleanup failure is not more important than the recovery failure.
297:     }
298:     return {
299:       procedureId: 'CG-RP-003',
300:       status: 'failed',
301:       quarantineDir,
302:       ...(knownGoodDir ? { knownGoodDir } : {}),
303:       restored,
304:       errors,
305:     };
306:   }
307: }
308: 
309: export function relativizeRecoveryPath(path: string, root: string = process.cwd()): string {
310:   const rel = relative(root, path);
311:   return rel.startsWith('..') ? path : rel;
312: }

2: // MODULE: Code Graph Database
3: // ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

5: // Uses separate code-graph.sqlite alongside the memory index DB.

14:   CODE_GRAPH_SCOPE_LABEL_KEY,

103: /** Schema version for migration tracking */

106: /** SQL schema for code graph tables */
107: const SCHEMA_SQL = `
108:   CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS code_files (
109:     id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,
110:     file_path TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE,
111:     language TEXT NOT NULL,
112:     content_hash TEXT NOT NULL,

121:   CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS code_nodes (

166:     file_path     TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
167:     error_class   TEXT NOT NULL CHECK (error_class IN ('B1', 'B2', 'OTHER')),
168:     error_message TEXT,

170:     last_seen_at  TEXT NOT NULL,
171:     attempt_count INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 1,
172:     source        TEXT NOT NULL CHECK (source IN ('seed', 'runtime'))
173:   );
174: 
175:   CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_nodes_file_id ON code_nodes(file_id);
176:   CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_nodes_symbol_id ON code_nodes(symbol_id);
177:   CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_nodes_kind ON code_nodes(kind);
178:   CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_nodes_name ON code_nodes(name);
179:   CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_file_line ON code_nodes(file_path, start_line);
180:   CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_edges_source ON code_edges(source_id, edge_type);
181:   CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_edges_target ON code_edges(target_id, edge_type);
182:   CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_edges_type ON code_edges(edge_type);
183:   CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_files_path ON code_files(file_path);
184:   CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_files_hash ON code_files(content_hash);
185:   CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_parse_diagnostics_last_seen ON parse_diagnostics(last_seen_at);
186:   CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_parser_skip_list_class ON parser_skip_list(error_class);
187: `;

189: function getCurrentFileMtimeMs(filePath: string): number | null {
190:   try {
191:     return Math.trunc(statSync(filePath).mtimeMs);
192:   } catch {
193:     return null;
194:   }
195: }
196: 
197: function getCurrentFileContentHash(filePath: string): string | null {
198:   try {
199:     return generateContentHash(readFileSync(filePath, 'utf-8'));
200:   } catch {
201:     return null;
202:   }
203: }
204: 
205: function hasColumn(database: Database.Database, tableName: string, columnName: string): boolean {
206:   const rows = database.prepare(`PRAGMA table_info(${tableName})`).all() as Array<{ name: string }>;
207:   return rows.some((row) => row.name === columnName);
208: }
209: 
210: function ensureSchemaMigrations(database: Database.Database): void {
211:   if (!hasColumn(database, 'code_files', 'file_mtime_ms')) {
212:     database.exec('ALTER TABLE code_files ADD COLUMN file_mtime_ms INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0');
213:   }
214: 
215:   database.exec(`
216:     CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS code_graph_metadata (
217:       key TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
218:       value TEXT NOT NULL,
219:       updated_at TEXT NOT NULL
220:     );
221:     CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS parse_diagnostics (
222:       file_path TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
223:       error_message TEXT NOT NULL,
224:       error_count INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 1,
225:       last_seen_at TEXT NOT NULL
226:     );
227:     CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS parser_skip_list (
228:       file_path     TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
229:       error_class   TEXT NOT NULL CHECK (error_class IN ('B1', 'B2', 'OTHER')),
230:       error_message TEXT,
231:       added_at      TEXT NOT NULL,
232:       last_seen_at  TEXT NOT NULL,
233:       attempt_count INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 1,
234:       source        TEXT NOT NULL CHECK (source IN ('seed', 'runtime'))
235:     );
236:     CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_file_line ON code_nodes(file_path, start_line);
237:     CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_parse_diagnostics_last_seen ON parse_diagnostics(last_seen_at);
238:     CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_parser_skip_list_class ON parser_skip_list(error_class);
239:   `);
240: 
241:   const now = new Date().toISOString();
242:   database.prepare(`
243:     INSERT OR IGNORE INTO parser_skip_list (
244:       file_path, error_class, error_message, added_at, last_seen_at, attempt_count, source
245:     )
246:     SELECT
247:       file_path,
248:       'B1',
249:       error_message,
250:       ?,
251:       ?,
252:       MAX(1, error_count),
253:       'seed'
254:     FROM parse_diagnostics
255:     WHERE error_message LIKE '%resolved is not a function%'
256:   `).run(now, now);
257: }
258: 
259: /** Initialize (or get) the code graph database */
260: export function initDb(dbDir: string): Database.Database {
261:   if (db) return db;
262: 
263:   try {
264:     dbPath = join(dbDir, 'code-graph.sqlite');
265:     db = new Database(dbPath);
266:     // Explicit busy_timeout so concurrent writers wait up to 5s
267:     // for the writer lock instead of throwing SQLITE_BUSY immediately. Set
268:     // BEFORE journal_mode/foreign_keys so any incidental contention during
269:     // PRAGMA setup also benefits from the wait.
270:     db.pragma('busy_timeout = 5000');
271:     db.pragma('journal_mode = WAL'); // WAL enables concurrent readers without locks
272:     db.pragma('foreign_keys = ON');
273:     db.exec(SCHEMA_SQL);
274:     ensureSchemaMigrations(db);
275: 
276:     const versionRow = db.prepare('SELECT version FROM schema_version LIMIT 1').get() as { version: number } | undefined;
277:     if (!versionRow) {
278:       db.prepare('INSERT INTO schema_version (version) VALUES (?)').run(SCHEMA_VERSION);
279:     } else if (versionRow.version < SCHEMA_VERSION) {
280:       db.prepare('UPDATE schema_version SET version = ?').run(SCHEMA_VERSION);
281:     }
282: 
283:     return db;
284:   } catch (err) {
285:     if (db) {
286:       try { db.close(); } catch { /* best effort cleanup for failed init */ }
287:     }
288:     db = null;
289:     dbPath = null;
290:     throw err;
291:   }

398: /**
399:  * Discriminated metadata read result so callers can distinguish
400:  * - 'absent': the row does not exist (first-write hasn't happened yet)
401:  * - 'resolved': the row exists, parsed cleanly, and matches the expected shape
402:  * - 'corrupt': the row exists but is not valid JSON (write-side bug or
403:  *   storage corruption)
404:  * - 'invalid-shape': the row parsed as JSON but does not match the expected
405:  *   shape (schema-version drift or write-side type bug)
406:  *
407:  * The original API (returns `null` on absent / corrupt / invalid-shape) is
408:  * preserved; new callers use these companions to react differently to corrupt
409:  * state vs absent state (e.g. quarantine the row instead of silently rebuilding).
410:  */
411: export type MetadataReadResult<T> =
412:   | { kind: 'absent' }
413:   | { kind: 'resolved'; value: T }
414:   | { kind: 'corrupt'; raw: string }
415:   | { kind: 'invalid-shape'; raw: string };
416: 
417: export function getLastDetectorProvenanceSummaryWithDiagnostics(): MetadataReadResult<DetectorProvenanceSummary> {
418:   // Typed read: distinguish absent vs corrupt vs invalid.
419:   const value = getMetadata('last_detector_provenance_summary');
420:   if (!value) {
421:     return { kind: 'absent' };
422:   }
423:   let parsed: unknown;
424:   try {
425:     parsed = JSON.parse(value);
426:   } catch {
427:     return { kind: 'corrupt', raw: value };
428:   }
429:   // Shape guard: must be an object with `dominant` and `counts` properties.
430:   if (
431:     typeof parsed !== 'object'
432:     || parsed === null
433:     || !('dominant' in parsed)
434:     || !('counts' in parsed)
435:   ) {
436:     return { kind: 'invalid-shape', raw: value };
437:   }
438:   return { kind: 'resolved', value: parsed as DetectorProvenanceSummary };
439: }
440:

442:   setMetadata('last_detector_provenance_summary', JSON.stringify(summary));
443: }

601:     )

683: /** Batch insert nodes for a file (deletes existing first) */
684: export function replaceNodes(fileId: number, nodes: CodeNode[]): void {
685:   const d = getDb();
686:   const insert = d.prepare(`
687:     INSERT OR IGNORE INTO code_nodes (symbol_id, file_id, file_path, fq_name, kind, name,
688:       start_line, end_line, start_column, end_column, language, signature, docstring, content_hash)
689:     VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?)
690:   `);
691: 
692:   const tx = d.transaction(() => {
693:     const oldSymbolRows = d.prepare('SELECT symbol_id FROM code_nodes WHERE file_id = ?').all(fileId) as { symbol_id: string }[];
694:     const oldSymbolIds = oldSymbolRows.map(row => row.symbol_id);
695: 
696:     d.prepare('DELETE FROM code_nodes WHERE file_id = ?').run(fileId);
697: 
698:     if (oldSymbolIds.length > 0) {
699:       const placeholders = oldSymbolIds.map(() => '?').join(',');
700:       d.prepare(`
701:         DELETE FROM code_edges
702:         WHERE source_id IN (${placeholders}) OR target_id IN (${placeholders})
703:       `).run(...oldSymbolIds, ...oldSymbolIds);
704:     }
705: 
706:     for (const n of nodes) {
707:       insert.run(
708:         n.symbolId, fileId, n.filePath, n.fqName, n.kind, n.name,
709:         n.startLine, n.endLine, n.startColumn, n.endColumn,
710:         n.language, n.signature ?? null, n.docstring ?? null, n.contentHash,
711:       );
712:     }
713:   });
714:   tx();
715: }

717: export interface ReplaceEdgesOptions {
718:   /**
719:    * BUG-03: when true, skip the inline dangling-target prune. During a full
720:    * scan, files are persisted one at a time; a cross-file IMPORTS edge whose
721:    * target lives in a not-yet-persisted file would otherwise be deleted here
722:    * (its `target_id` is not yet in `code_nodes`) and is never restored, because
723:    * the post-persist resolver only reconciles CALLS edges. Callers that persist
724:    * many files in sequence pass `true` and run `pruneDanglingEdges()` ONCE after
725:    * all nodes + cross-file resolution land. Selective/single-file callers leave
726:    * it false so genuinely-dangling edges are cleaned inline.
727:    */
728:   deferDanglingTargetPrune?: boolean;
729: }
730: 
731: /** Batch insert edges (deletes edges from the source nodes first) */
732: export function replaceEdges(sourceIds: string[], edges: CodeEdge[], opts: ReplaceEdgesOptions = {}): void {
733:   const d = getDb();
734: 
735:   const insert = d.prepare(`
736:     INSERT INTO code_edges (source_id, target_id, edge_type, weight, metadata)
737:     VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, ?)
738:   `);
739: 
740:   const tx = d.transaction(() => {
741:     if (sourceIds.length > 0) {
742:       const placeholders = sourceIds.map(() => '?').join(',');
743:       d.prepare(`DELETE FROM code_edges WHERE source_id IN (${placeholders})`).run(...sourceIds);
744:     }
745: 
746:     const candidateSourceIds = [...new Set(edges.map((edge) => edge.sourceId))];
747:     const retainedSourceIds = new Set<string>();
748:     if (candidateSourceIds.length > 0) {
749:       const placeholders = candidateSourceIds.map(() => '?').join(',');
750:       const rows = d.prepare(`
751:         SELECT symbol_id
752:         FROM code_nodes
753:         WHERE symbol_id IN (${placeholders})
754:       `).all(...candidateSourceIds) as Array<{ symbol_id: string }>;
755:       for (const row of rows) {
756:         retainedSourceIds.add(row.symbol_id);
757:       }
758:     }
759: 
760:     for (const e of edges) {
761:       if (!retainedSourceIds.has(e.sourceId)) {
762:         continue;
763:       }
764:       insert.run(e.sourceId, e.targetId, e.edgeType, e.weight, e.metadata ? JSON.stringify(e.metadata) : null);
765:     }
766: 
767:     if (!opts.deferDanglingTargetPrune) {
768:       d.prepare(`
769:         DELETE FROM code_edges WHERE
770:           source_id NOT IN (SELECT symbol_id FROM code_nodes) OR
771:           target_id NOT IN (SELECT symbol_id FROM code_nodes)
772:       `).run();
773:     }
774:   });
775:   tx();
776: }
777: 
778: /**
779:  * BUG-03: remove edges whose source or target symbol no longer exists in
780:  * `code_nodes`. Run ONCE after a multi-file scan (all nodes persisted +
781:  * cross-file resolution complete) when per-file `replaceEdges` deferred its
782:  * inline prune. Returns the number of edges removed.
783:  */
784: export function pruneDanglingEdges(): number {
785:   const d = getDb();
786:   const result = d.prepare(`
787:     DELETE FROM code_edges WHERE
788:       source_id NOT IN (SELECT symbol_id FROM code_nodes) OR
789:       target_id NOT IN (SELECT symbol_id FROM code_nodes)
790:   `).run();
791:   return result.changes;
792: }

794: /** Check if a file needs re-indexing based on stored mtime and content hash */
795: export function isFileStale(filePath: string, options?: { currentContentHash?: string }): boolean {
796:   const d = getDb();
797:   const row = d.prepare('SELECT file_mtime_ms, content_hash FROM code_files WHERE file_path = ?').get(filePath) as {
798:     file_mtime_ms: number;
799:     content_hash: string | null;
800:   } | undefined;
801:   if (!row) return true;
802:   const currentMtimeMs = getCurrentFileMtimeMs(filePath);
803:   if (currentMtimeMs === null) return true;
804:   if (!row.content_hash) return true;
805: 
806:   // Hash content on mtime drift before declaring stale. A touch
807:   // (mtime drift, content unchanged) used to force reindex; now it stays
808:   // fresh as long as the content hash matches. Avoids gratuitous full-scans
809:   // on `git checkout` of unchanged files.
810:   const currentContentHash = options?.currentContentHash ?? getCurrentFileContentHash(filePath);
811:   if (currentContentHash === null) return true;
812:   return row.content_hash !== currentContentHash;
813: }
814: 
815: /** Batch stale check for a set of file paths */
816: export interface FreshFilesResult {
817:   readonly stale: string[];
818:   readonly fresh: string[];
819: }
820: 
821: export function ensureFreshFiles(filePaths: string[]): FreshFilesResult {
822:   const uniquePaths = [...new Set(filePaths)];
823:   if (uniquePaths.length === 0) {
824:     return { stale: [], fresh: [] };
825:   }
826: 
827:   const d = getDb();
828:   const placeholders = uniquePaths.map(() => '?').join(',');
829:   const rows = d.prepare(`
830:     SELECT file_path, file_mtime_ms, content_hash
831:     FROM code_files
832:     WHERE file_path IN (${placeholders})
833:   `).all(...uniquePaths) as Array<{ file_path: string; file_mtime_ms: number; content_hash: string | null }>;
834:   const storedFiles = new Map(rows.map((row) => [row.file_path, row]));
835: 
836:   const stale: string[] = [];
837:   const fresh: string[] = [];
838: 
839:   for (const filePath of uniquePaths) {
840:     const storedFile = storedFiles.get(filePath);
841:     const currentMtimeMs = getCurrentFileMtimeMs(filePath);
842:     if (storedFile === undefined || currentMtimeMs === null) {
843:       stale.push(filePath);
844:       continue;
845:     }
846:     if (!storedFile.content_hash) {
847:       stale.push(filePath);
848:       continue;
849:     }
850:     // Hash on mtime drift before declaring stale. Touch-only
851:     // changes (mtime drift, content unchanged) stay fresh; only real content
852:     // changes flip the file to stale.
853:     const currentContentHash = getCurrentFileContentHash(filePath);
854:     if (currentContentHash === null || storedFile.content_hash !== currentContentHash) {
855:       stale.push(filePath);
856:       continue;
857:     }
858:     fresh.push(filePath);
859:   }
860: 
861:   return { stale, fresh };
862: }

1223:  * value into `code_edges.metadata`.
1224:  *
1225:  * Allowlist rules (single line, length-cap, non-control-char):

1264:     const meta = rawMetadata as Record<string, unknown>;
1265:     if ('reason' in meta) {

1267:     }
1268:     if ('step' in meta) {

1: // ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
2: // MODULE: Canonical Code Graph DB Directory
3: // ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
4: 
5: import { existsSync, mkdirSync, realpathSync } from 'node:fs';
6: import { dirname, resolve } from 'node:path';
7: import { isWithinWorkspace } from './utils/workspace-path.js';
8: 
9: export class CanonicalDbDirError extends Error {
10:   readonly code: string | undefined;
11: 
12:   constructor(message: string, code?: string) {
13:     super(message);
14:     this.name = 'CanonicalDbDirError';
15:     this.code = code;
16:   }
17: }
18: 
19: export function resolveCanonicalDbDir(dir: string, workspaceRoot?: string): string {
20:   const resolvedDir = resolve(dir);
21: 
22:   try {
23:     let canonicalWorkspace: string | null = null;
24:     if (workspaceRoot) {
25:       const resolvedWorkspace = resolve(workspaceRoot);
26:       if (!isWithinWorkspace(resolvedWorkspace, resolvedDir)) {
27:         throw new CanonicalDbDirError(
28:           `Code graph DB directory must stay within the workspace root: ${resolvedWorkspace}`,
29:           'OUTSIDE_WORKSPACE',
30:         );
31:       }
32:       canonicalWorkspace = realpathSync.native(resolvedWorkspace);
33:     }
34:     // Resolve symlinks of the deepest EXISTING ancestor and reject an out-of-workspace
35:     // escape BEFORE mkdir, so a symlink-escaping path never creates directories outside the
36:     // workspace. (The lexical pre-check above does not resolve symlinks; the post-mkdir check
37:     // below stays as defense-in-depth against a TOCTOU symlink swap.)
38:     if (canonicalWorkspace) {
39:       let ancestor = resolvedDir;
40:       while (!existsSync(ancestor)) {
41:         const parent = dirname(ancestor);
42:         if (parent === ancestor) break;
43:         ancestor = parent;
44:       }
45:       const canonicalAncestor = realpathSync.native(ancestor);
46:       if (!isWithinWorkspace(canonicalWorkspace, canonicalAncestor)) {
47:         throw new CanonicalDbDirError(
48:           `Code graph DB directory must stay within the workspace root: ${canonicalWorkspace}`,
49:           'OUTSIDE_WORKSPACE',
50:         );
51:       }
52:     }
53:     mkdirSync(resolvedDir, { recursive: true, mode: 0o700 });
54:     const canonicalDir = realpathSync.native(resolvedDir);
55:     if (canonicalWorkspace && !isWithinWorkspace(canonicalWorkspace, canonicalDir)) {
56:       throw new CanonicalDbDirError(
57:         `Code graph DB directory must stay within the workspace root: ${canonicalWorkspace}`,
58:         'OUTSIDE_WORKSPACE',
59:       );
60:     }
61:     return canonicalDir;
62:   } catch (error: unknown) {
63:     if (error instanceof CanonicalDbDirError) {
64:       throw error;
65:     }
66:     const code = error && typeof error === 'object' && 'code' in error
67:       ? String(error.code)
68:       : undefined;
69:     const message = error instanceof Error ? error.message : String(error);
70:     throw new CanonicalDbDirError(`Unable to resolve canonical code graph DB directory ${resolvedDir}: ${message}`, code);
71:   }
72: }

42:   freshness: GraphFreshness;
43:   action: ReadyAction;
44:   files?: string[];
45:   inlineIndexPerformed: boolean;
46:   reason: string;
47:   activeScope?: ReadinessScopeDiagnostic;
48:   storedScope?: ReadinessScopeDiagnostic;
49:   manifestCount?: number | null;
50:   manifestDigest?: string | null;
51:   parseErrorBacklog?: number;
52:   autoRescanSafety?: 'allowed' | 'blocked';
53:   autoRescanBlockReason?: string;
54:   selfHealAttempted?: boolean;
55:   selfHealResult?: 'ok' | 'failed' | 'skipped';
56:   verificationGate?: 'pass' | 'fail' | 'absent';
57:   lastSelfHealAt?: string;
58: }
59: 
60: export interface EnsureReadyOptions {
61:   allowInlineIndex?: boolean;
62:   allowInlineFullScan?: boolean;
63:   allowGuardedInlineFullScan?: boolean;
64:   parseErrorBacklogThreshold?: number;
65: }

67: // ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
68: // Constants
69: // ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
70: 
71: /** Maximum time (ms) before auto-indexing is aborted */
72: const AUTO_INDEX_TIMEOUT_MS = 10_000;
73: 
74: /**
75:  * Maximum stale files before we switch from selective to full reindex.
76:  *
77:  * Operators can tune via `SPECKIT_CODE_GRAPH_SELECTIVE_REINDEX_THRESHOLD`
78:  * (positive integer). Default `50` balances incremental-rescan speed
79:  * (fast for small diffs) against full-scan throughput (one expensive pass
80:  * for bulk changes). Raise to delay full-scans (favors session-boot
81:  * latency over freshness). Lower to keep the DB tighter at the cost of
82:  * more frequent full-scans.
83:  */
84: function resolveSelectiveReindexThreshold(): number {
85:   const raw = process.env.SPECKIT_CODE_GRAPH_SELECTIVE_REINDEX_THRESHOLD;
86:   if (typeof raw !== 'string' || raw.trim() === '') return 50;
87:   const parsed = Number.parseInt(raw.trim(), 10);
88:   return Number.isFinite(parsed) && parsed > 0 ? parsed : 50;
89: }
90: export const SELECTIVE_REINDEX_THRESHOLD = resolveSelectiveReindexThreshold();
91: const GUARDED_FULL_SCAN_PARSE_ERROR_THRESHOLD = 0;
92: 
93: // ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
94: // Internal helpers
95: // ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
96: 
97: function getCurrentGitHead(rootDir: string): string | null {
98:   try {
99:     return execFileSync('git', ['rev-parse', 'HEAD'], {
100:       cwd: rootDir,
101:       encoding: 'utf-8',
102:       timeout: 5_000,
103:       stdio: ['ignore', 'pipe', 'pipe'],
104:     }).trim();
105:   } catch {
106:     return null;
107:   }
108: }
109: 
110: function isCommitSha(value: string): boolean {
111:   return /^[0-9a-fA-F]{40}$/.test(value);
112: }
113: 
114: /**
115:  * Return the file-paths touched by `git diff` between two HEAD shas.
116:  * Returns null on git failure (caller falls back to the existing
117:  * full-scan behavior — no regression). Returns an empty array when both shas
118:  * are valid but no files changed between them.
119:  *
120:  * The diff is `--name-only` and includes added/modified/deleted/renamed paths
121:  * so the caller can intersect against `getTrackedFiles()` to determine
122:  * whether a HEAD pointer change actually affects the indexed set.
123:  */
124: function getGitDiffFilePaths(rootDir: string, fromSha: string, toSha: string): string[] | null {
125:   if (!isCommitSha(fromSha) || !isCommitSha(toSha)) {
126:     return null;
127:   }
128:   try {
129:     const out = execFileSync('git', ['diff', '--name-only', `${fromSha}..${toSha}`], {
130:       cwd: rootDir,
131:       encoding: 'utf-8',
132:       timeout: 5_000,
133:       stdio: ['ignore', 'pipe', 'pipe'],
134:     });
135:     return out.split('\n').map((line) => line.trim()).filter((line) => line.length > 0);
136:   } catch {
137:     return null;
138:   }
139: }
140: 
141: /**
142:  * Decide whether a raw HEAD pointer change actually touches the indexed file
143:  * set. Returns:
144:  *   - 'unknown' if git is unavailable or one sha is missing (caller keeps
145:  *     existing full-scan behavior so we never silently downgrade safety)
146:  *   - 'in-scope' if the diff touches at least one tracked file
147:  *   - 'out-of-scope' if the diff touches no tracked files (HEAD pointer can be
148:  *     advanced without a full reindex)
149:  */
150: function classifyHeadDriftScope(
151:   rootDir: string,
152:   storedHead: string | null,
153:   currentHead: string | null,
154:   trackedFiles: string[],
155: ): 'unknown' | 'in-scope' | 'out-of-scope' {
156:   if (!storedHead || !currentHead || storedHead === currentHead) {
157:     return 'unknown';
158:   }
159:   const diffPaths = getGitDiffFilePaths(rootDir, storedHead, currentHead);
160:   if (diffPaths === null) {
161:     return 'unknown';
162:   }
163:   if (diffPaths.length === 0) {
164:     return 'out-of-scope';
165:   }
166:   // Intersect with tracked files. Compare against both bare paths (relative
167:   // to rootDir) and absolute paths since `getTrackedFiles()` may store either.
168:   const trackedSet = new Set(trackedFiles);
169:   for (const candidate of diffPaths) {
170:     if (trackedSet.has(candidate)) return 'in-scope';
171:     // Try absolute resolution (rootDir + candidate)
172:     const absCandidate = candidate.startsWith('/') ? candidate : `${rootDir.replace(/\/$/, '')}/${candidate}`;
173:     if (trackedSet.has(absCandidate)) return 'in-scope';
174:   }
175:   return 'out-of-scope';
176: }
177: 
178: // ─── Candidate manifest for tracked-set drift detection ───
179: //
180: // BUG-02 (corrected scope): this manifest pins the cardinality + digest of the
181: // CURRENTLY-TRACKED file set (`getTrackedFiles()` == rows in `code_files`). It is
182: // recorded from the tracked set after a scan and compared against the tracked
183: // set on `detectState()`, so it detects changes WITHIN the tracked universe
184: // (e.g. a bulk rebuild that rewrites the tracked set, or tracked-file removals)
185: // and complements the per-file mtime check.
186: //
187: // It does NOT detect brand-new UNTRACKED files: a file that has never been
188: // indexed is absent from `getTrackedFiles()` at both record and compare time,
189: // so it cannot diverge the manifest. Detecting brand-new on-disk files at read
190: // time would require a filesystem walk of the include globs, which the read
191: // path deliberately avoids (NFR-P01: no FS walk on the read path). Brand-new
192: // files are picked up on the next explicit/triggered full `code_graph_scan`,
193: // which walks the include globs itself.
194: //
195: // Bounded: persists `{count, digest}` only (no per-path storage) so 10k+ file
196: // monorepos don't bloat the metadata table.
197: 
198: const CANDIDATE_MANIFEST_KEY = 'candidate_manifest';
199: 
200: interface CandidateManifest {
201:   readonly count: number;
202:   readonly digest: string;
203:   readonly recordedAt: string;
204: }
205: 
206: export interface ReadinessScopeDiagnostic {
207:   readonly fingerprint: string | null;
208:   readonly label: string | null;
209:   readonly source: string | null;
210: }
211: 
212: function scopeDiagnostic(scope: {
213:   fingerprint?: string | null;
214:   label?: string | null;
215:   source?: string | null;
216: }): ReadinessScopeDiagnostic {
217:   return {
218:     fingerprint: scope.fingerprint ?? null,
219:     label: scope.label ?? null,
220:     source: scope.source ?? null,
221:   };
222: }
223: 
224: function buildReadinessDiagnostics(): Pick<
225:   ReadyResult,
226:   'activeScope' | 'storedScope' | 'manifestCount' | 'manifestDigest' | 'parseErrorBacklog'
227: > {
228:   const activeScope = resolveIndexScopePolicy();
229:   const storedScope = graphDb.getStoredCodeGraphScope();
230:   const manifest = loadCandidateManifest();
231:   let parseErrorBacklog = 0;
232:   try {
233:     parseErrorBacklog = graphDb.getParseDiagnosticsSummary().affectedFiles;
234:   } catch {
235:     parseErrorBacklog = 0;
236:   }
237: 
238:   return {
239:     activeScope: scopeDiagnostic(activeScope),
240:     storedScope: scopeDiagnostic(storedScope),
241:     manifestCount: manifest?.count ?? null,
242:     manifestDigest: manifest?.digest ?? null,
243:     parseErrorBacklog,
244:   };
245: }
246: 
247: function evaluateGuardedFullScan(
248:   diagnostics: ReturnType<typeof buildReadinessDiagnostics>,
249:   parseErrorBacklogThreshold: number,
250: ): Pick<ReadyResult, 'autoRescanSafety' | 'autoRescanBlockReason'> {
251:   // Delegate to the shared `auto-rescan-policy.ts` helper so this gate and
252:   // the read-path handler gate stay in sync.
253:   const decision = shouldAutoRescan({
254:     storedScope: { fingerprint: diagnostics.storedScope?.fingerprint ?? null },
255:     activeScope: { fingerprint: diagnostics.activeScope?.fingerprint ?? null },
256:     parseDiagnosticsBacklog: diagnostics.parseErrorBacklog ?? 0,
257:     parseDiagnosticsBacklogThreshold: parseErrorBacklogThreshold,
258:   });
259:   if (!decision.allowed) {
260:     return {
261:       autoRescanSafety: 'blocked',
262:       autoRescanBlockReason: decision.blockReason,
263:     };
264:   }
265:   return { autoRescanSafety: 'allowed' };
266: }
267: 
268: /**
269:  * Load the persisted candidate manifest. Returns null if no manifest exists
270:  * (first run) or if the stored value is malformed.
271:  */
272: function loadCandidateManifest(): CandidateManifest | null {
273:   // Bounded read of the persisted manifest.
274:   const raw = graphDb.getCodeGraphMetadata(CANDIDATE_MANIFEST_KEY);
275:   if (!raw) return null;
276:   try {
277:     const parsed = JSON.parse(raw) as unknown;
278:     if (!isRecord(parsed)) return null;
279:     if (typeof parsed.count !== 'number' || typeof parsed.digest !== 'string' || typeof parsed.recordedAt !== 'string') {
280:       return null;
281:     }
282:     return { count: parsed.count, digest: parsed.digest, recordedAt: parsed.recordedAt };
283:   } catch {
284:     return null;
285:   }
286: }
287: 
288: /**
289:  * Persist a candidate manifest. Called after any successful full or selective
290:  * scan so the next `detectState()` has a baseline to compare against.
291:  */
292: export function recordCandidateManifest(filePaths: string[]): void {
293:   // Persist {count, digest} only — no per-path storage.
294:   const sorted = [...filePaths].sort();
295:   const digest = createHash('sha256').update(sorted.join('\n'), 'utf-8').digest('hex').slice(0, 16);
296:   const manifest: CandidateManifest = {
297:     count: sorted.length,
298:     digest,
299:     recordedAt: new Date().toISOString(),
300:   };
301:   graphDb.setCodeGraphMetadata(CANDIDATE_MANIFEST_KEY, JSON.stringify(manifest));
302: }
303: 
304: /**
305:  * Compare on-disk tracked file paths against the stored candidate manifest.
306:  * Returns true when divergence is detected (count differs OR digest differs);
307:  * false when the manifest matches OR no manifest exists yet.
308:  *
309:  * Bounded compare: no filesystem walk happens here — we use the same
310:  * `getTrackedFiles()` set that `detectState()` already loads, plus existence
311:  * checks already performed by `partitionTrackedFiles()`. The manifest pins the
312:  * cardinality + digest of the TRACKED set so a bulk rebuild that rewrites that
313:  * set (or removes tracked files) flips to stale even when per-file mtimes look
314:  * unchanged. Note (BUG-02): brand-new UNTRACKED files are NOT detected here —
315:  * they are absent from `getTrackedFiles()`, so they cannot diverge the digest;
316:  * they are indexed by the next full `code_graph_scan` (which walks the globs).
317:  */
318: function detectCandidateManifestDrift(filePaths: string[]): boolean {
319:   const stored = loadCandidateManifest();
320:   if (!stored) return false; // no baseline = no drift signal yet
321:   const sorted = [...filePaths].sort();
322:   if (sorted.length !== stored.count) return true;
323:   const digest = createHash('sha256').update(sorted.join('\n'), 'utf-8').digest('hex').slice(0, 16);
324:   return digest !== stored.digest;
325: }
326: 
327: function partitionTrackedFiles(filePaths: string[]): { existingFiles: string[]; deletedFiles: string[] } {
328:   const existingFiles: string[] = [];
329:   const deletedFiles: string[] = [];
330: 
331:   for (const filePath of filePaths) {
332:     if (existsSync(filePath)) {
333:       existingFiles.push(filePath);
334:       continue;
335:     }
336: 
337:     deletedFiles.push(filePath);
338:   }
339: 
340:   return { existingFiles, deletedFiles };
341: }
342: 
343: function cleanupDeletedTrackedFiles(filePaths: string[]): number {
344:   for (const filePath of filePaths) {
345:     graphDb.removeFile(filePath);
346:   }
347: 
348:   return filePaths.length;
349: }
350: 
351: function appendCleanupReason(reason: string, removedDeletedCount: number): string {
352:   if (removedDeletedCount === 0) {
353:     return reason;
354:   }
355: 
356:   return `${reason}; removed ${removedDeletedCount} deleted tracked file(s)`;
357: }
358: 
359: function getVerificationGate(
360:   verification: object | null,
361: ): ReadyResult['verificationGate'] {
362:   if (!isRecord(verification)) {
363:     return 'absent';
364:   }
365: 
366:   if (verification.passed === false) {
367:     return 'fail';
368:   }
369: 
370:   if (!isRecord(verification.pass_policy)) {
371:     return verification.passed === true ? 'pass' : 'absent';
372:   }
373: 
374:   const overallThreshold = verification.pass_policy.overall_pass_rate;
375:   const edgeThreshold = verification.pass_policy.edge_focus_pass_rate;
376:   const overallPassRate = verification.overall_pass_rate ?? verification.overallPassRate;
377:   const edgeFocusPassRate = verification.edge_focus_pass_rate;
378: 
379:   if (
380:     typeof overallThreshold !== 'number'
381:     || typeof edgeThreshold !== 'number'
382:     || typeof overallPassRate !== 'number'
383:     || typeof edgeFocusPassRate !== 'number'
384:   ) {
385:     return verification.passed === true ? 'pass' : 'absent';
386:   }
387: 
388:   return overallPassRate >= overallThreshold && edgeFocusPassRate >= edgeThreshold
389:     ? 'pass'
390:     : 'fail';
391: }

393: /** Detect graph state without triggering any reindex */
394: function detectState(rootDir: string): {
395:   freshness: GraphFreshness;
396:   action: ReadyAction;
397:   staleFiles: string[];
398:   deletedFiles: string[];
399:   reason: string;
400: } {
401:   const d = getDb();
402: 
403:   // Condition (a): Graph is empty
404:   const nodeCount = (d.prepare('SELECT COUNT(*) as c FROM code_nodes').get() as { c: number }).c;
405:   if (nodeCount === 0) {
406:     return { freshness: 'empty', action: 'full_scan', staleFiles: [], deletedFiles: [], reason: 'graph is empty (0 nodes)' };
407:   }
408: 
409:   const activeScope = resolveIndexScopePolicy();
410:   const storedScope = graphDb.getStoredCodeGraphScope();
411:   // FIX-009-v3: env-vs-stored drift only blocks reads when the prior scan
412:   // took its policy from env (`env`/`default`). When the prior scan was an
413:   // explicit per-call override (`scan-argument`), the index contains exactly
414:   // what the user just asked for — trust it and let reads proceed regardless
415:   // of env drift. This preserves the FIX-009-v2 cross-session env-change
416:   // contract while restoring read-after-scan semantics for explicit probes.
417:   const storedFromPerCall = storedScope.source === 'scan-argument';
418:   if (!storedFromPerCall && !scopeFingerprintsMatchOrLegacy(storedScope.fingerprint, activeScope.fingerprint)) {
419:     const storedLabel = storedScope.label ?? 'unknown previous code graph scope';
420:     return {
421:       freshness: 'stale',
422:       action: 'full_scan',
423:       staleFiles: [],
424:       deletedFiles: [],
425:       reason: `code graph scope changed: stored=${storedLabel}; active=${activeScope.label}; run code_graph_scan with incremental:false`,
426:     };
427:   }
428: 
429:   // Condition (b): Git HEAD changed
430:   const currentHead = getCurrentGitHead(rootDir);
431:   const storedHead = getLastGitHead();
432:   const headChanged = Boolean(currentHead && storedHead && currentHead !== storedHead);
433:   // headChanged proves both git refs are present before formatting the transition.
434:   const headChangedReason = headChanged
435:     ? `git HEAD changed: ${storedHead!.slice(0, 8)} -> ${currentHead!.slice(0, 8)}`
436:     : null;
437: 
438:   // Condition (c): Check file mtime drift on tracked files
439:   const trackedFiles = graphDb.getTrackedFiles();
440:   if (trackedFiles.length === 0) {
441:     return { freshness: 'empty', action: 'full_scan', staleFiles: [], deletedFiles: [], reason: 'no tracked files in code_files table' };
442:   }
443: 
444:   // Classify HEAD drift by index scope. Raw HEAD drift no longer
445:   // triggers full_scan if the diff touches no path in `getTrackedFiles()`.
446:   // 'unknown' means git was unavailable or shas are missing — we keep the
447:   // existing safe behavior (treat headChanged as significant).
448:   const headScope = headChanged
449:     ? classifyHeadDriftScope(rootDir, storedHead, currentHead, trackedFiles)
450:     : 'unknown';
451:   const headChangedSignificant = headChanged && headScope !== 'out-of-scope';
452: 
453:   const { existingFiles, deletedFiles } = partitionTrackedFiles(trackedFiles);
454:   const { stale } = ensureFreshFiles(existingFiles);
455: 
456:   // Detect untracked-indexable drift via the candidate manifest.
457:   // If the on-disk indexable cardinality or digest diverges from the stored
458:   // baseline, flip to stale + full_scan even when individual mtimes look fine.
459:   const manifestDrift = detectCandidateManifestDrift(trackedFiles);
460: 
461:   if (stale.length === 0) {
462:     if (headChangedSignificant) {
463:       return {
464:         freshness: 'stale',
465:         action: 'full_scan',
466:         staleFiles: [],
467:         deletedFiles,
468:         reason: deletedFiles.length > 0
469:           ? `${headChangedReason}; tracked files appear up-to-date on disk; ${deletedFiles.length} tracked file(s) no longer exist on disk`
470:           : `${headChangedReason}; tracked files appear up-to-date on disk`,
471:       };
472:     }
473: 
474:     if (manifestDrift) {
475:       return {
476:         freshness: 'stale',
477:         action: 'full_scan',
478:         staleFiles: [],
479:         deletedFiles,
480:         reason: 'candidate manifest drift: indexable file set has changed since last scan',
481:       };
482:     }
483: 
484:     if (deletedFiles.length > 0) {
485:       return {
486:         freshness: 'stale',
487:         action: 'none',
488:         staleFiles: [],
489:         deletedFiles,
490:         reason: `${deletedFiles.length} tracked file(s) no longer exist on disk`,
491:       };
492:     }
493: 
494:     // HEAD pointer may have advanced without touching tracked
495:     // files. Update the stored HEAD so we don't re-classify on every probe.
496:     if (headChanged && headScope === 'out-of-scope' && currentHead) {
497:       setLastGitHead(currentHead);
498:     }
499:     return { freshness: 'fresh', action: 'none', staleFiles: [], deletedFiles: [], reason: 'all tracked files are up-to-date' };
500:   }
501: 
502:   // Too many stale files => full scan is more efficient
503:   if (stale.length > SELECTIVE_REINDEX_THRESHOLD) {
504:     return {
505:       freshness: 'stale',
506:       action: 'full_scan',
507:       staleFiles: stale,
508:       deletedFiles,
509:       reason: [
510:         headChangedReason,
511:         `${stale.length} stale files exceed selective threshold (${SELECTIVE_REINDEX_THRESHOLD})`,
512:         deletedFiles.length > 0 ? `${deletedFiles.length} tracked file(s) no longer exist on disk` : null,
513:       ].filter(Boolean).join('; '),
514:     };
515:   }
516: 
517:   return {
518:     freshness: 'stale',
519:     action: 'selective_reindex',
520:     staleFiles: stale,
521:     deletedFiles,
522:     reason: [
523:       headChangedReason,
524:       `${stale.length} file(s) have newer mtime than indexed_at`,
525:       deletedFiles.length > 0 ? `${deletedFiles.length} tracked file(s) no longer exist on disk` : null,
526:     ].filter(Boolean).join('; '),
527:   };
528: }

530: /** Run indexFiles with a timeout guard */
531: async function indexWithTimeout(
532:   config: IndexerConfig,
533:   timeoutMs: number,
534:   indexOptions?: Parameters<typeof indexFiles>[1],
535: ): Promise<void> {
536:   const controller = new AbortController();
537:   const timer = setTimeout(() => controller.abort(), timeoutMs);
538: 
539:   try {
540:     const results = await Promise.race([
541:       // BUG-06: pass the deadline signal so indexFiles' phase runner can stop
542:       // between phases on timeout instead of running to completion in the
543:       // background and discarding the result.
544:       indexFiles(config, { ...indexOptions, signal: controller.signal }),
545:       new Promise<never>((_, reject) => {
546:         controller.signal.addEventListener('abort', () =>
547:           reject(new Error(`Auto-indexing timed out after ${timeoutMs}ms`)),
548:         );
549:       }),
550:     ]);
551: 
552:     let persistedDetectorProvenance: ReturnType<typeof graphDb.getLastDetectorProvenance> = null;
553:     for (const result of results) {
554:       try {
555:         persistIndexedFileResult(result);
556:         persistedDetectorProvenance ??= result.detectorProvenance;
557:       } catch {
558:         // Best-effort: skip files that fail to persist.  File remains stale
559:         // (mtime=0 from Stage 1) so the next scan will retry structural rows.
560:       }
561:     }
562: 
563:     if (persistedDetectorProvenance) {
564:       graphDb.setLastDetectorProvenance(persistedDetectorProvenance);
565:     }
566:   } finally {
567:     clearTimeout(timer);
568:   }
569: }
570: 
571: /**
572:  * Persist one indexed file without marking it fresh until structural rows land.
573:  *
574:  * Stage `file_mtime_ms=0`, write nodes/edges, then finalize with the real
575:  * mtime so persistence failures leave the file stale for retry.
576:  *
577:  * Wrap the four storage operations (stage upsert, replaceNodes, replaceEdges,
578:  * finalize upsert) in a single per-file transaction so a crash mid-persistence
579:  * rolls all four back atomically. Per-file scope keeps the
580:  * lock window short (3-4 statements) so concurrent readers/writers on the
581:  * same DB are not starved during a long scan.
582:  */
583: export function persistIndexedFileResult(
584:   result: ParseResult,
585:   opts: { deferDanglingTargetPrune?: boolean } = {},
586: ): void {
587:   if (result.parseHealth === 'error') {
588:     graphDb.recordParseDiagnostic(result.filePath, result.parseErrors.join('; '));
589:     return;
590:   }
591: 
592:   // Atomic per-file persistence boundary.
593:   const tx = graphDb.getDb().transaction(() => {
594:     const fileId = graphDb.upsertFile(
595:       result.filePath, result.language, result.contentHash,
596:       result.nodes.length, result.edges.length,
597:       result.parseHealth, result.parseDurationMs,
598:       { fileMtimeMs: 0 },
599:     );
600:     graphDb.replaceNodes(fileId, result.nodes);
601:     const sourceIds = result.nodes.map((node) => node.symbolId);
602:     graphDb.replaceEdges(sourceIds, result.edges, opts);
603:     graphDb.upsertFile(
604:       result.filePath, result.language, result.contentHash,
605:       result.nodes.length, result.edges.length,
606:       result.parseHealth, result.parseDurationMs,
607:     );
608:     graphDb.clearParseDiagnostic(result.filePath);
609:   });
610:   tx();
611: }

613: // ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
614: // Public API
615: // ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
616: 
617: /**
618:  * Check whether the code graph is ready and, if not, perform
619:  * the minimum necessary reindexing before returning.
620:  *
621:  * Has a 10-second timeout guard so auto-indexing never blocks
622:  * queries forever.
623:  */
624: export async function ensureCodeGraphReady(rootDir: string, options: EnsureReadyOptions = {}): Promise<ReadyResult> {
625:   const allowInlineIndex = options.allowInlineIndex ?? true;
626:   const allowInlineFullScan = options.allowInlineFullScan ?? allowInlineIndex;
627:   const allowGuardedInlineFullScan = options.allowGuardedInlineFullScan ?? false;
628:   const parseErrorBacklogThreshold = options.parseErrorBacklogThreshold ?? GUARDED_FULL_SCAN_PARSE_ERROR_THRESHOLD;
629: 
630:   const state = detectState(rootDir);
631:   const removedDeletedCount = cleanupDeletedTrackedFiles(state.deletedFiles);
632:   const verificationGate = getVerificationGate(graphDb.getLastGoldVerification());
633:   const diagnostics = buildReadinessDiagnostics();
634: 
635:   if (state.action === 'none') {
636:     return {
637:       freshness: state.freshness,
638:       action: 'none',
639:       inlineIndexPerformed: false,
640:       reason: appendCleanupReason(state.reason, removedDeletedCount),
641:       ...diagnostics,
642:       verificationGate,
643:     };
644:   }
645: 
646:   if (state.action === 'selective_reindex' && !allowInlineIndex) {
647:     return {
648:       freshness: state.freshness,
649:       action: state.action,
650:       ...(state.action === 'selective_reindex' ? { files: state.staleFiles } : {}),
651:       inlineIndexPerformed: false,
652:       reason: appendCleanupReason(`${state.reason}; inline auto-index skipped for read path`, removedDeletedCount),
653:       ...diagnostics,
654:       selfHealAttempted: true,
655:       selfHealResult: 'skipped',
656:       verificationGate,
657:     };
658:   }
659: 
660:   // First-time auto-establish: an EMPTY graph has nothing to overwrite, so a
661:   // first full scan is safe to auto-run on a read path WITHOUT an explicit
662:   // code_graph_scan — but only under the DEFAULT end-user-code scope. A fresh
663:   // clone (default scope) gets "it just works"; a maintainer who opted .opencode
664:   // in (a large scope) keeps the explicit gate so a quick query never silently
665:   // triggers a big all-of-.opencode scan. The normal scope-fingerprint guard
666:   // (evaluateGuardedFullScan) still protects POPULATED/stale graphs from unsafe
667:   // scope-mismatched auto-rescans.
668:   const firstTimeAutoEstablish = state.freshness === 'empty'
669:     && allowGuardedInlineFullScan
670:     && isDefaultEndUserScope(resolveIndexScopePolicy());
671: 
672:   const guardedFullScan = firstTimeAutoEstablish
673:     ? { autoRescanSafety: 'allowed' as const }
674:     : state.action === 'full_scan' && allowGuardedInlineFullScan
675:       ? evaluateGuardedFullScan(diagnostics, parseErrorBacklogThreshold)
676:       : { autoRescanSafety: 'blocked' as const, autoRescanBlockReason: 'guard_disabled' };
677:   const canRunFullScan = allowInlineFullScan || guardedFullScan.autoRescanSafety === 'allowed';
678:   if (state.action === 'full_scan' && !canRunFullScan) {
679:     return {
680:       freshness: state.freshness,
681:       action: state.action,
682:       inlineIndexPerformed: false,
683:       reason: appendCleanupReason(`${state.reason}; inline full scan skipped for read path`, removedDeletedCount),
684:       ...diagnostics,
685:       ...guardedFullScan,
686:       verificationGate,
687:     };
688:   }
689: 
690:   // Honor the last explicit scan's stored scope so per-call disabled scans are
691:   // not silently re-broadened back to env-resolved scope at read time. Without
692:   // this, a `code_graph_scan({includeSkills:false,...})` followed by any read
693:   // re-resolves scope from env (which may say `INDEX_*=true`), detects a
694:   // mismatch, and immediately blocks reads with `requiredAction:"code_graph_scan"`.
695:   const storedScope = graphDb.getStoredCodeGraphScope();
696:   const storedPolicy = parseIndexScopePolicyFromFingerprint({
697:     fingerprint: storedScope.fingerprint,
698:     source: storedScope.source,
699:   });
700: 
701:   try {
702:     if (state.action === 'full_scan') {
703:       const config = getDefaultConfig(rootDir, storedPolicy ?? undefined);
704:       await indexWithTimeout(config, AUTO_INDEX_TIMEOUT_MS);
705:       graphDb.setCodeGraphScope(config.scopePolicy);
706: 
707:       // Update stored git HEAD after full scan
708:       const head = getCurrentGitHead(rootDir);
709:       if (head) setLastGitHead(head);
710:       // Refresh candidate manifest after a full scan so the next detectState()
711:       // has a current baseline to compare against.
712:       try {
713:         recordCandidateManifest(graphDb.getTrackedFiles());
714:       } catch {
715:         // Best-effort: manifest recording must never block a successful scan
716:       }
717: 
718:       const refreshedState = detectState(rootDir);
719:       return {
720:         freshness: refreshedState.freshness,
721:         action: refreshedState.action,
722:         ...(refreshedState.action === 'selective_reindex' ? { files: refreshedState.staleFiles } : {}),
723:         inlineIndexPerformed: true,
724:         reason: appendCleanupReason(refreshedState.reason, removedDeletedCount),
725:         ...buildReadinessDiagnostics(),
726:         ...(state.action === 'full_scan' && allowGuardedInlineFullScan
727:           ? { ...guardedFullScan, selfHealAttempted: true, selfHealResult: 'ok' as const }
728:           : {}),
729:         verificationGate,
730:       };
731:     }
732: 
733:     // selective_reindex: only re-parse stale files
734:     if (state.action === 'selective_reindex' && state.staleFiles.length > 0) {
735:       const lastSelfHealAt = new Date().toISOString();
736:       const config = getDefaultConfig(rootDir, storedPolicy ?? undefined);
737:       await indexWithTimeout(config, AUTO_INDEX_TIMEOUT_MS, { specificFiles: state.staleFiles });
738:       graphDb.setCodeGraphScope(config.scopePolicy);
739: 
740:       const head = getCurrentGitHead(rootDir);
741:       if (head) setLastGitHead(head);
742:       // Refresh candidate manifest after a selective reindex too
743:       // (the tracked-file set may have grown if the scan added new files).
744:       try {
745:         recordCandidateManifest(graphDb.getTrackedFiles());
746:       } catch {
747:         // Best-effort: manifest recording must never block a successful scan
748:       }
749: 
750:       const refreshedState = detectState(rootDir);
751:       const selfHealResult = refreshedState.freshness === 'fresh' && refreshedState.action === 'none'
752:         ? 'ok'
753:         : 'failed';
754:       return {
755:         freshness: refreshedState.freshness,
756:         action: refreshedState.action,
757:         ...(refreshedState.action === 'selective_reindex' ? { files: refreshedState.staleFiles } : {}),
758:         inlineIndexPerformed: true,
759:         reason: appendCleanupReason(refreshedState.reason, removedDeletedCount),
760:         ...buildReadinessDiagnostics(),
761:         selfHealAttempted: true,
762:         selfHealResult,
763:         verificationGate,
764:         lastSelfHealAt,
765:       };
766:     }
767:   } catch (err: unknown) {
768:     const msg = err instanceof Error ? err.message : String(err);
769:     console.error(`[ensure-ready] Auto-index failed: ${msg}`);
770:     return {
771:       freshness: state.freshness,
772:       action: state.action,
773:       files: state.staleFiles,
774:       inlineIndexPerformed: false,
775:       reason: appendCleanupReason(`${state.reason} (auto-index failed: ${msg})`, removedDeletedCount),
776:       ...diagnostics,
777:       verificationGate,
778:       ...(state.action === 'selective_reindex'
779:         ? {
780:             selfHealAttempted: true,
781:             selfHealResult: 'failed' as const,
782:             lastSelfHealAt: new Date().toISOString(),
783:           }
784:         : {}),
785:     };
786:   }
787: 
788:   return {
789:     freshness: state.freshness,
790:     action: 'none',
791:     inlineIndexPerformed: false,
792:     reason: appendCleanupReason(state.reason, removedDeletedCount),
793:     ...diagnostics,
794:     verificationGate,
795:   };
796: }
797: 
798: /**
799:  * Non-mutating freshness check for status display.
800:  * Does NOT trigger reindexing.
801:  *
802:  * Returns 'error' on probe crash so callers can canonically distinguish
803:  * "scope is unreachable" from "scope is empty".
804:  * The widened union flows through readiness-contract → trustStateFromGraphState
805:  * to emit `trustState: 'unavailable'` per the widened contract.
806:  */
807: export function getGraphFreshness(rootDir: string): GraphFreshness {
808:   try {
809:     const state = detectState(rootDir);
810:     return state.freshness;
811:   } catch {
812:     return 'error';
813:   }
814: }
815: 
816: // ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
817: // Read-only readiness snapshot
818: // ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
819: // Surfaces the same `action` + `freshness` + `reason` triplet that
820: // `ensureCodeGraphReady` would emit, but WITHOUT mutating any state:
821: //   - no debounce cache writes
822: //   - no cleanup of deleted tracked files
823: //   - no inline indexing (full or selective)
824: //   - no `setLastGitHead` updates
825: //
826: // Used by `code_graph_status` so operators can distinguish
827: // "needs full scan" vs "needs selective reindex" vs "fresh"
828: // without invoking `code_graph_scan` (which mutates).
829: 
830: export interface GraphReadinessSnapshot {
831:   freshness: GraphFreshness;
832:   action: ReadyAction;
833:   reason: string;
834: }
835: 
836: /**
837:  * Compute the readiness action that `ensureCodeGraphReady` WOULD take,
838:  * without performing any mutation. Safe to call from `code_graph_status`
839:  * and any other read-only diagnostic surface.
840:  *
841:  * Reuses the detection-only branch of {@link detectState}; intentionally
842:  * does NOT touch the cache (`readinessDebounce`), the deleted-file cleanup
843:  * (`cleanupDeletedTrackedFiles`), or the inline indexer.
844:  *
845:  * On probe crash, returns `{ freshness: 'error', action: 'none', reason }`
846:  * so the consumer can render an unavailable state instead of inheriting an
847:  * exception.
848:  */
849: export function getGraphReadinessSnapshot(rootDir: string): GraphReadinessSnapshot {
850:   try {
851:     const state = detectState(rootDir);
852:     return {
853:       freshness: state.freshness,
854:       action: state.action,
855:       reason: state.reason,
856:     };
857:   } catch (err: unknown) {
858:     const msg = err instanceof Error ? err.message : String(err);
859:     return {
860:       freshness: 'error',
861:       action: 'none',
862:       reason: `readiness probe crashed: ${msg}`,
863:     };
864:   }
865: }

157: export function classifyApplyState(input: ApplyStateInput): ApplyClassification {
158:   const reasons: string[] = [];
159:   const staleFileCount = input.staleFileCount ?? input.staleFiles?.length ?? 0;
160:   const schemaMismatch = input.expectedSchemaVersion !== undefined
161:     && input.schemaVersion !== undefined

164:     ? input.verification.passed === false
165:     || input.verification.overall_pass_rate < input.verification.pass_policy.overall_pass_rate
166:     || input.verification.edge_focus_pass_rate < input.verification.pass_policy.edge_focus_pass_rate
167:     : false;

169:   if (input.freshness === 'empty' || input.freshness === 'error') {
170:     reasons.push(`freshness=${input.freshness}`);
171:   }
172:   if (input.trustState === 'absent' || input.trustState === 'unavailable') {
173:     reasons.push(`trustState=${input.trustState}`);
174:   }
175:   if (input.gitHeadDrift === true) {
176:     reasons.push('git HEAD drift detected');
177:   }
178:   if (staleFileCount > 50) {
179:     reasons.push(`${staleFileCount} stale files exceed soft-stale boundary`);
180:   }
181:   if (schemaMismatch) {

185:     reasons.push('missing lastPersistedAt');

248: async function dispatchOperation(
249:   operation: ApplyOperation,
250:   args: CodeGraphApplyArgs,
251:   classification: ApplyClassification,
252:   options: ApplyOrchestratorOptions,

254:   recovery?: RecoveryProcedureResult;
255:   excludeRules?: ClassifiedExcludeRule[];

260: 
261:   switch (operation) {
262:     case 'rescan':
263:       await scan({
264:         rootDir: args.rootDir,
265:         incremental: classification.scanIncremental ?? false,
266:       });
267:       return {};
268:     case 'recover-sqlite-corruption':
269:       return {
270:         recovery: await recoverSqliteCorruption({
271:           dbDir,
272:           auditDir: options.auditDir,
273:           now: options.now,
274:           scan: (scanArgs) => scan({ rootDir: args.rootDir, incremental: scanArgs.incremental }),
275:         }),
276:       };
277:     case 'rollback-bad-apply':
278:       return {
279:         recovery: await rollbackBadApply({
280:           dbDir,
281:           auditDir: options.auditDir,
282:           now: options.now,
283:           scan: (scanArgs) => scan({ rootDir: args.rootDir, incremental: scanArgs.incremental }),
284:         }),
285:       };
286:     case 'prune-excludes': {
287:       const artifactPath = options.excludeRuleConfidencePath;
288:       const patterns = args.excludePatterns ?? [];
289:       if (!artifactPath) {
290:         return { excludeRules: patterns.map((pattern) => ({ pattern, tier: 'unknown' })) };
291:       }
292:       const classified = classifyExcludeRules(loadExcludeRuleConfidence(artifactPath), patterns);
293:       const mediumBlocked = classified.some((entry) => entry.tier === 'medium') && args.confirm !== true;
294:       const lowBlocked = classified.some((entry) => entry.tier === 'low') && args.lowTierOptIn !== true;
295:       if (mediumBlocked) {
296:         throw new Error('medium-tier exclude patterns require confirm=true');
297:       }
298:       if (lowBlocked) {
299:         throw new Error('low-tier exclude patterns require lowTierOptIn=true');
300:       }
301:       const excludeGlobs = classified
302:         .filter((entry) => entry.tier === 'high' || entry.tier === 'medium' || entry.tier === 'low')
303:         .map((entry) => entry.pattern);
304:       if (excludeGlobs.length > 0) {
305:         await scan({
306:           rootDir: args.rootDir,
307:           incremental: false,
308:           excludeGlobs,
309:         });
310:       }
311:       return { excludeRules: classified };
312:     }
313:     case 'repair-nodes': {
314:       if (args.crashRootCauseAddressed !== true) {
315:         return {
316:           repairNodes: {
317:             eligible: [],
318:             skippedReason: 'crashRootCauseAddressed is not true',
319:           },
320:         };
321:       }
322:       const thresholdMs = (args.quarantineOlderThanDays ?? DEFAULT_QUARANTINE_AGE_DAYS) * 24 * 60 * 60 * 1000;
323:       const cutoff = Date.now() - thresholdMs;
324:       const rows = graphDb.getDb().prepare(`
325:         SELECT file_path, last_seen_at
326:         FROM parser_skip_list
327:         ORDER BY last_seen_at ASC, file_path ASC
328:         LIMIT 50
329:       `).all() as Array<{ file_path: string; last_seen_at: string }>;
330:       const eligible = rows
331:         .filter((row) => {
332:           const seenAt = Date.parse(row.last_seen_at);
333:           return Number.isFinite(seenAt) && seenAt <= cutoff;
334:         })
335:         .map((row) => row.file_path);
336:       if (eligible.length > 0) {
337:         if (args.confirm !== true) {
338:           throw new Error('repair-nodes requires confirm=true');
339:         }
340:         await scan({ rootDir: args.rootDir, incremental: true });
341:       }
342:       return { repairNodes: { eligible } };
343:     }
344:     default:
345:       throw new Error(`Unsupported apply operation: ${operation satisfies never}`);
346:   }
347: }

354:   persistApplyMetadata({
355:     status: result.status,
356:     batteryPassRate: result.battery?.overall_pass_rate ?? null,
357:     now: result.now,
358:   });
359: }
360: 
361: export async function applyCodeGraph(
362:   args: CodeGraphApplyArgs = {},
363:   options: ApplyOrchestratorOptions = {},
364: ): Promise<ApplyRunResult> {
365:   const now = options.now ?? (() => new Date());
366:   const logPath = auditLogPath(options);
367:   const runBattery = options.battery ?? (() => runGoldBattery({
368:     batteryPath: args.batteryPath ?? DEFAULT_GOLD_BATTERY_PATH,
369:     includeDetails: args.includeDetails,
370:   }));
371: 
372:   const preflight = await runBattery();
373:   appendAudit(logPath, 'preflight_battery', summarizeBattery(preflight), now);
374:   if (!preflight.passed) {
375:     const classification = classifyApplyState({ verification: preflight });
376:     recordApplyMetadata({ status: 'aborted', battery: preflight, now });
377:     appendAudit(logPath, 'abort', { reason: 'preflight battery failed' }, now);
378:     return {
379:       status: 'aborted',
380:       operation: args.operation ?? 'rescan',
381:       classification,
382:       auditLogPath: logPath,
383:       preflight,
384:       message: 'Pre-flight gold battery failed; apply aborted before mutation.',
385:     };
386:   }
387: 
388:   const statusInput = await (options.status ?? defaultStatus)();
389:   const classification = classifyApplyState({
390:     ...statusInput,
391:     verification: statusInput.verification ?? preflight,
392:   });
393:   const operation = chooseOperation(args.operation, classification);
394:   appendAudit(logPath, 'classification', {
395:     state: classification.state,
396:     reasons: classification.reasons,
397:     operation,
398:   }, now);
399: 
400:   if (classification.state === 'fresh' && args.operation === undefined) {
401:     const postflight = await runBattery();
402:     appendAudit(logPath, 'postflight_battery', summarizeBattery(postflight), now);
403:     recordApplyMetadata({ status: args.dryRun ? 'dry-run' : 'noop', battery: postflight, now });
404:     return {
405:       status: args.dryRun ? 'dry-run' : 'noop',
406:       operation,
407:       classification,
408:       auditLogPath: logPath,
409:       preflight,
410:       postflight,
411:       message: 'Graph is already fresh; no apply operation was needed.',
412:     };
413:   }
414: 
415:   if (classification.state === 'hard-stale' && args.confirm !== true) {
416:     recordApplyMetadata({ status: 'aborted', battery: preflight, now });
417:     appendAudit(logPath, 'abort', {
418:       reason: 'hard-stale requires confirm=true',
419:       hardStaleReasons: classification.reasons,
420:     }, now);
421:     return {
422:       status: 'aborted',
423:       operation,
424:       classification,
425:       auditLogPath: logPath,
426:       preflight,
427:       message: 'Hard-stale recovery requires confirm=true before mutation.',
428:     };
429:   }
430: 
431:   if (args.dryRun === true) {
432:     const postflight = await runBattery();
433:     appendAudit(logPath, 'postflight_battery', summarizeBattery(postflight), now);
434:     recordApplyMetadata({ status: 'dry-run', battery: postflight, now });
435:     return {
436:       status: 'dry-run',
437:       operation,
438:       classification,
439:       auditLogPath: logPath,
440:       preflight,
441:       postflight,
442:       message: 'Dry run completed; operation dispatch was skipped.',
443:     };
444:   }
445: 
446:   const knownGoodDir = snapshotKnownGoodTriplet({
447:     dbDir: options.dbDir,
448:     auditDir: options.auditDir,
449:     now,
450:   });
451:   appendAudit(logPath, 'known_good_snapshot', { knownGoodDir }, now);
452: 
453:   let dispatchResult: Awaited<ReturnType<typeof dispatchOperation>>;
454:   try {
455:     dispatchResult = await dispatchOperation(operation, args, classification, options);
456:     appendAudit(logPath, 'operation_dispatched', { operation, dispatchResult }, now);
457:   } catch (error) {
458:     const recovery = await rollbackBadApply({
459:       dbDir: options.dbDir,
460:       auditDir: options.auditDir,
461:       now,
462:       scan: (scanArgs) => (options.scan ?? runDefaultScan)({ rootDir: args.rootDir, incremental: scanArgs.incremental }),
463:     });
464:     appendAudit(logPath, 'rollback', {
465:       reason: error instanceof Error ? error.message : String(error),
466:       recovery,
467:     }, now);
468:     recordApplyMetadata({ status: 'rolled-back', battery: preflight, now });
469:     return {
470:       status: 'rolled-back',
471:       operation,
472:       classification,
473:       auditLogPath: logPath,
474:       preflight,
475:       recovery,
476:       message: 'Apply operation failed; rollback executed.',
477:     };
478:   }
479: 
480:   const postflight = await runBattery();
481:   appendAudit(logPath, 'postflight_battery', summarizeBattery(postflight), now);
482: 
483:   if (!postflight.passed) {
484:     const recovery = await rollbackBadApply({
485:       dbDir: options.dbDir,
486:       auditDir: options.auditDir,
487:       now,
488:       scan: (scanArgs) => (options.scan ?? runDefaultScan)({ rootDir: args.rootDir, incremental: scanArgs.incremental }),
489:     });
490:     appendAudit(logPath, 'rollback', {
491:       reason: 'postflight battery failed',
492:       recovery,
493:     }, now);
494:     recordApplyMetadata({ status: 'rolled-back', battery: postflight, now });
495:     return {
496:       status: 'rolled-back',
497:       operation,
498:       classification,
499:       auditLogPath: logPath,
500:       preflight,
501:       postflight,
502:       recovery,
503:       ...dispatchResult,
504:       message: 'Post-flight gold battery failed; rollback executed.',
505:     };
506:   }
507: 
508:   recordApplyMetadata({ status: 'committed', battery: postflight, now });
509:   appendAudit(logPath, 'commit', { operation }, now);
510:   return {
511:     status: 'committed',
512:     operation,
513:     classification,
514:     auditLogPath: logPath,
515:     preflight,
516:     postflight,
517:     ...dispatchResult,
518:     message: 'Apply operation committed after post-flight gold battery passed.',
519:   };
520: }
521: 
522: export function buildReadinessForApplyState(input: ApplyStateInput) {
523:   return buildReadinessBlock({
524:     freshness: input.freshness === 'fresh' || input.freshness === 'stale' || input.freshness === 'empty' || input.freshness === 'error'
525:       ? input.freshness
526:       : 'error',
527:     action: input.action === 'none' || input.action === 'selective_reindex' || input.action === 'full_scan'
528:       ? input.action
529:       : 'none',
530:     inlineIndexPerformed: false,
531:     reason: input.freshness ? `apply state freshness=${input.freshness}` : 'apply state unavailable',
532:   });
533: }
